description:
A researcher has asked for your help to prepare a clean, reproducible Python-package
    environment for analysing several CSV datasets that live in
    /home/user/datasets.  
    Follow these steps and document everything so the automation team can
    verify success.

    1. Create a virtual environment named “research_env” directly inside the existing
       directory /home/user/datasets  
       (i.e. the virtual-environment root folder must be
       /home/user/datasets/research_env).

    2. Inside /home/user/datasets write a file called
       requirements.txt that contains **exactly** the three lines
          numpy==1.24.3
          pandas==1.5.3
          matplotlib==3.7.1
       (nothing before, after or between those lines; respect *case* and order).

    3. Activate the virtual environment and install everything in
       requirements.txt using pip.

    4. After installation, create a plain-text log file named
       /home/user/datasets/research_env_install.log whose contents are
       **exactly** the following seven lines (no extra blank lines or spaces):

          PACKAGE_FREEZE_OUTPUT_START
          matplotlib==3.7.1
          numpy==1.24.3
          pandas==1.5.3
          PACKAGE_FREEZE_OUTPUT_END

       The automated checker will compare this file byte-for-byte, so make sure
       the spelling, capitalisation, order of packages and the two sentinel
       lines are identical.

    Verification details the grader will look for:
    • Directory /home/user/datasets/research_env exists.  
    • File /home/user/datasets/research_env/bin/activate exists (shows the venv
      was actually created).  
    • File /home/user/datasets/requirements.txt exists with the three exact
      lines shown above.  
    • File /home/user/datasets/research_env_install.log exists *and* its entire
      contents match the 7-line block shown in step 4, with no deviations.

    You only need a handful of typical shell commands (creating the venv,
    echo/printf or a text editor to build the two files, and pip install).
    Do **not** remove or rename any existing material in /home/user/datasets.
truth:
The grader will assert:

    1. Path “/home/user/datasets/research_env” is a directory.

    2. Path “/home/user/datasets/research_env/bin/activate” is a regular file
       (the virtual-environment activation script).

    3. File “/home/user/datasets/requirements.txt” exists and its complete,
       exact contents (including trailing newline) are:
          numpy==1.24.3\n
          pandas==1.5.3\n
          matplotlib==3.7.1\n

    4. File “/home/user/datasets/research_env_install.log” exists and its
       complete, exact contents (including trailing newline) are:
          PACKAGE_FREEZE_OUTPUT_START\n
          matplotlib==3.7.1\n
          numpy==1.24.3\n
          pandas==1.5.3\n
          PACKAGE_FREEZE_OUTPUT_END\n
